theme: Skia m151 and allocation-free hot paths
highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 upgrades to Skia m151 and delivers allocation-free HarfBuzz and SKShaper hot paths.
highlights_body: The Skia engine moves to milestone m151 with multiple rounds of upstream bug fixes. Several high-frequency HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper code paths are reworked to eliminate per-call heap allocations, reducing GC pressure in tight render loops. WASM gains Emscripten 5.0.6 support for .NET 11, and a CPU raster regression is corrected.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine is now built from chrome/m151, followed by three additional rounds of upstream bug-fix syncs, pulling in stability and correctness improvements from the Skia team.
SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char> in addition to string, eliminating substring allocations when parsing colours from slices or stack-allocated buffers.

An upstream change removed SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT, silently switching the CPU raster pipeline to a slower path with incorrect output in some cases. The flag has been restored, recovering both correctness and performance on the CPU raster backend.

bullets.lead: Native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData fixed
bullets.detail: A code path in SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) was leaking a native reference when a subset image could not be created. The handle is now released correctly.

bullets.lead: Unnecessary unsafe modifier removed from GRContextOptions
bullets.detail: GRContextOptions no longer declares an unsafe context where none was needed, reducing compiler noise in projects that treat unsafe as a warning.

bullets.lead: HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper hot paths made allocation-free
bullets.detail: HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encoder instead of allocating a byte array per call. HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse eliminate a per-call char[4] allocation. SKShaper reads glyph results via zero-copy spans instead of copying into intermediate arrays. Together these changes cut GC pressure significantly in text-heavy render loops.

bullets.lead: WASM gains Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries for .NET 11
bullets.detail: SkiaSharp now ships Emscripten 5.0.6 libraries targeting .NET 11 WASM, and the Skia submodule is updated to drop a redefined SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM macro that broke WASM builds.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 support for .NET 11 and the Skia submodule fix that eliminated the SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 delivered the allocation-free HarfBuzz and SKShaper hot paths, the SKColor ReadOnlySpan overloads, the CPU raster regression fix, the native resource leak fix in SKImage, and the WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 support.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151 line with the Skia m151 milestone upgrade and the initial WASM submodule fix.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 14.2.1 ships with this release. The WASM libraries are refreshed for Emscripten 5.0.6 and .NET 11, and two high-frequency Tag.Parse and Buffer.AddUtf8 code paths are made allocation-free.
